titleStmt.title: SII 25.39: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 39. (A.R. No. 39 of 1908.) VARICHCHIYŪR, MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a rocky outcrop of the village Kuṉṉattūr, three furlongs south of the village.

This undated inscription can be palaeographically assigned to 15th century A.D. It seems to record a gift of three villages viz., Ma[lai]-kkuḍi, Palāṅguḷam and Añ[ja]ru as dēvadāna to the deity Vīrabhadra-nāyaṉār, by Vīramarasayya, the kaṟtar of Madurai-maṇḍalam, for the merit of Nāgaṇanāyakkar.
Vīramarasayya of this record is evidently identical with Ayyaṉ Vīramarasa figuring in a record from Dārāśuram. (A.R.Ep., No. 22 of 1908)

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
ciṟiman ma(ā)kāmaṇṭalīcuraṉ metiṉi
micurakaṇṭa kaṭṭāri cāḷuva Immaṭi nara
ciṅkamakārāyar piṟativirācciyam ceytaru
ḷukiṟa nākaṇa nāyakkaṟku taṉmamāka maturai
maṇṭalattukku kaṟtarāṉa viramaracayyaṉ varuccūr
Read virapattira jirapattira nāyaṉār teyvatāṉamāka
viṭṭa malaikkuṭi palāṅkuḷam Añcarumā
yitta muṉṟu Entalyuḷ paṭṭamunṟumāRest of the record is lost.
Digital edition of SII 25.39 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
